channel.title: NYT > World News
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Trump Escalates Threat to Hit Iranian Power Plants After U.S. Rescues Downed Airman
item.description: President Trump used an expletive-laden social media post to taunt Iranian leaders, saying that the United States would attack if they did not fully reopen the Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times

item.title: Pepsi Drops Sponsorship of Wireless Festival Headlined by Kanye West
item.description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer said he was “deeply concerned” that the rapper known for antisemitic and racist comments had been booked to perform at the Wireless Festival.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear
item.media:description: Kanye West in Milan in 2024.

item.title: Pope Leo XIV Calls for Peace in First Easter Mass
item.description: The pontiff’s Easter remarks follow a Palm Sunday homily in which he said God rejected the prayers of “those who wage war.”
item.dc:creator: Elisabetta Povoledo
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Pope Leo XIV arriving on Sunday in St. Peter’s Square for Easter Mass, during which he renewed his pleas for peace and condemned violence.

item.title: More Than 70 Migrants Missing After Boat Capsizes Off Libya
item.description: Survivors said at least 100 people were on a boat that had left Libya for Europe. Only 32 of those aboard are known to have survived.
item.dc:creator: Elisabetta Povoledo
item.media:credit: Fabian Melber/Sea-Watch, via Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: A photograph released by the nongovernmental organization Sea-Watch showing migrants on a capsized boat off Libya on Saturday.

item.title: Iran’s Downing of Fighter Jet and U.S. Rescue Leave Both Sides Dangerously Emboldened
item.description: After Iran shot down a U.S. plane and U.S. forces pulled off a risky ground operation to extricate a stranded airman, both sides claimed victory. That confidence could fuel further escalation.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: An anti-U.S. billboard on Sunday in Tehran showing American aircraft captured in a net.

item.title: China Built the World’s Drone Industry. Now It’s Locking Down the Skies.
item.description: The Chinese government tightened rules to curb what it described as illegal drone use, but some users say the changes are now restricting too many flights.
item.dc:creator: Joy Dong
item.media:description: A delivery drone last month above Shenzhen, China. China has sharply tightened rules for the use of recreational and civilian drones.

item.title: What We Know About the Rescue of a U.S. Airman After Two Jet Crashes
item.description: An airman was rescued on Saturday night, President Trump said. The rescue came after a risky two-day operation deep inside Iran.
item.dc:creator: Neil Vigdor, Aurelien Breeden and Yan Zhuang
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: An F-15E Strike Eagle, the type of plane shot down in Iran.

item.title: Interceptor Missiles Save Lives, but Stockpiles Are Dwindling
item.description: Defense systems that shoot projectiles out of the sky have become one of the most important components of warfare, so much so that militaries face a diminishing supply.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Kulish
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Manama, Bahrain, after a drone was intercepted near the Bahrain Financial Harbour towers last month.

item.title: Dozens Killed in Haiti Massacre as International Force Trickles In
item.description: Gangs tore through several rural communities last weekend, underscoring the challenges that will face the new, U.N.-backed Gang Suppression Force starting to enter the country.
item.dc:creator: André Paultre and Frances Robles

item.title: Deadly Earthquake and Floods Worsen Afghanistan’s Troubles
item.description: Floods have killed at least 77 people this week and destroyed or damaged thousands of homes, and an earthquake on Friday killed a dozen more.
item.dc:creator: Yaqoob Akbary
item.category: United States Agency for International Development
item.media:description: Afghans beside a flooded road in Herat Province on Thursday.

item.title: Trump Wants to Make Deportation Deals. Autocrats Are Ready to Listen.
item.description: The White House has turned deportations, a signature domestic issue, into a major piece of foreign policy. Here’s what we know about the program.
item.dc:creator: Eileen Sullivan, Hamed Aleaziz, Megha Rajagopalan and Pranav Baskar
item.category: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump and Secretary of State Marco Rubio last month at the White House.

item.title: Europe’s Options in the Strait of Hormuz: Few, and Risky
item.description: European leaders and other officials have ideas for bringing shipping back to the strait once the Iran war ends. But none of them are sure bets.
item.dc:creator: Jim Tankersley
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Cargo ships near the Strait of Hormuz last month.

item.title: Three Charged With Arson in Attack on U.K. Jewish Charity’s Ambulances
item.description: A fourth suspect was arrested after showing up at a hearing for the defendants, the police said. The March attack was widely condemned but has not been declared a terrorist incident.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Castle
item.media:description: The aftermath of the March 23 arson attack, which took place outside a synagogue in north London.

item.title: Epstein Presented Himself to Indian Tycoon as a Trump White House Insider
item.description: The convicted sex offender gave Anil Ambani information on appointments and foreign policy. Some seemed prescient, though there was no evidence he was close to the administration.
item.dc:creator: Anupreeta Das
item.media:description: Anil Ambani, an Indian businessman who exchanged emails with Jeffrey Epstein for more than two years, in 2022 in Mumbai.

item.title: This Is Not China’s War, but Beijing Started Preparing for It Years Ago
item.description: Long concerned about geopolitical crises, China redoubled efforts to secure energy security when President Trump started raising the stakes in his first term.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra Stevenson and Murphy Zhao
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Oil storage tanks and facilities of a Sinopec plant in Shanghai last month.

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Sunday
item.description: President Trump taunted Iran on social media, while strikes continued in Iran, Israel, Lebanon and some Gulf states.
item.dc:creator: Eve Sampson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Avishag Shaar-Yashuv for The New York Times
item.media:description: Emergency medical workers search through the rubble of a residential building struck by an Iranian missile attack on Sunday in Haifa, Israel.

item.title: A Looming Deadline
item.description: We look at the options for reopening the Strait of Hormuz as President Trump escalates his threats.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Here’s a Timeline of Trump’s Ultimatums Over the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: President Trump first gave Iran 48 hours to reopen the vital oil shipping route on March 21. The deadline has been reset many times since.
item.dc:creator: Anushka Patil
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries
item.media:description: Boats in the Strait of Hormuz in March. Transit through the waterway has slowed to a trickle since the start of the conflict.

item.title: Trump Revels in Threats to Commit War Crimes in Iran
item.description: The president said he would bomb Iran “back to the Stone Ages.” Until this administration, American leaders had insisted they were trying to follow international law in war.
item.dc:creator: Edward Wong
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump wrote on social media that “all Hell will reign down” on Iran unless it opens the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: How the CIA Helped Locate a Missing Airman After Iran Downed a U.S. Fighter Jet
item.description: The intelligence agency also executed a deception campaign aimed at drawing Iranian forces away from where the missing Air Force officer was hiding.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A still image from a video of U.S. aircraft over southwest Iran on Friday.

item.title: Former Matador Is Gored to Death Before Annual Bullfight in Spain
item.description: The accident happened before the Corrida Picassiana, an annual event in Malaga that honors the painter Pablo Picasso.
item.dc:creator: Claire Moses
item.media:description: A matador performing a pass during the Corrida Picassiana in Malaga in 2024.

item.title: Trump Seems Emboldened With New Threats in Truth Social Post Over Strait of Hormuz
item.description: In an expletive-filled social media post, Mr. Trump said Iran should open the Strait of Hormuz or he will bomb bridges and power plants.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity
item.media:description: President Trump at the White House last week.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman, Greg Jaffe, Helene Cooper, Eric Schmitt and David E. Sanger

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Saturday
item.description: The U.S. military’s search-and-rescue operation for a missing American airman entered its second day. The Israeli military struck a major petrochemical complex in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Ahn
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: The site of a strike at Shahid Beheshti University in Tehran on Saturday.

item.title: Israel Strikes Iran’s Largest Petrochemical Complex
item.description: The sprawling complex in the city of Mahshahr was taken offline after strikes hit two utility plants that provided basic services like gas and power, Iranian officials said.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The Mahshahr petrochemical complex in 2011.

item.title: Satellite image company restricts Mideast content, citing a U.S. government request.
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar
item.media:credit: Planet Labs PBC, via Associated Press
item.media:description: A satellite photo of Kharg Island in Iran in February that was distributed by Planet Labs.

item.title: Here’s how two airmen would have ejected from the F-15E.
item.description: A step-by-step look at the process by which the two crew members probably ejected from the fighter jet.
item.dc:creator: Mika Gröndahl, Lily Boyce and Thomas Gibbons-Neff

item.title: U.S. Fighter Jet Downed Over Iran Was Probably Based at R.A.F. Lakenheath, U.K. Airfield
item.description: A military analyst identified markings consistent with a squadron based at R.A.F. Lakenheath, one of two British bases that host the largest U.S. fighter jet operation in Europe.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Castle
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: A fighter jet at R.A.F. Lakenheath in Mildenhall, England, in January.

item.title: A projectile hit near Iran’s Bushehr nuclear plant, killing a worker.
item.description: There was no reported increase in radiation levels. A string of recent attacks near the Persian Gulf facility has fueled fears of a nuclear accident.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward
item.media:description: A satellite image of the nuclear power plant in Bushehr, Iran, in December.

item.title: Missing Airman Raises Concerns That Iran Could Gain Leverage Over the U.S.
item.description: Since 1979, Iran has repeatedly used Americans and Europeans detained on its territory to win concessions over more powerful adversaries.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Women walking by a mural of the Statue of Liberty on the outer wall of the former U.S. Embassy in Tehran. In 1979, a group of militant students took over the embassy and kept 52 Americans captive for 444 days.

item.title: Living Space
item.description: We don’t have to travel to the moon to gain some of the insight that astronauts do.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Kirsch

item.title: Stephen Lewis Was a Singular Man on a Mission
item.description: Heartbreaking scenes of death from H.I.V. in Africa enraged and fueled the work of the Canadian AIDS activist and politician who died on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Nolen

item.title: Iranians Seek Respite in Nature Amid the War
item.description: Families gathered for picnics and games this week to mark the end of the Iranian new year holiday, seizing on a brief chance to celebrate.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati and Arash Khamooshi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: People gathered across Tehran on Thursday to celebrate Sizdah Bedar, the end of the two-week holiday celebrating the Iranian new year.

item.title: Trump Pledged a Quick End to the Iran War, but He Hasn’t Explained How
item.description: President Trump bet that American firepower could cow Iran into compliance. So far, Iran’s leaders have been unwilling to quit.
item.dc:creator: Greg Jaffe
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Neither President Trump nor Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has been able to explain how the war, now in its fifth week, will end.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Greg Jaffe, Eric Schmitt and Helene Cooper

item.title: ‘Iran Is Being Destroyed in Front of Our Eyes’: Tehran Is Gripped by Fear
item.description: Fifteen residents of Tehran said in telephone interviews and text messages that the capital was weathering heavy bombardment.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Residents in front of buildings damaged by airstrikes in Karaj, Iran, on Friday.

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Friday
item.description: An American fighter jet was shot down by Iran for the first time since the start of the war, leading to an urgent search-and-rescue operation for a missing crew member.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Ahn
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Amit Elkayam for The New York Times
item.media:description: A home in Ramat Gan, Israel, damaged by a projectile during an Iranian missile strike on Friday.

item.title: Ex-US Army Pilot Recalls Battle for Survival When Shot Down in Iraq
item.description: Ronald Young Jr. was in the Army when his Apache Longbow copter went down during the U.S. invasion of Iraq.
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Ronald Young Jr. boarding a transport plane near Baghdad in 2003, after escaping captivity.

item.title: Iran’s Defenses Have Been Struck, but They Can Still Fire Missiles and Drones
item.description: Iran has sent waves of missiles and drones at Israel and Gulf states in recent weeks and shot down an American fighter jet on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Kulish
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Planet Labs Pbc/Planet Labs PBC, via Associated Press
item.media:description: This satellite photo shows damage after an Iranian attack at Al Udeid Air Base in Qatar last month. Iran struck the regional air headquarters of U.S. Central Command, damaging an early-warning radar system.

item.title: In the Philippines, Rising Fuel Prices Force Travelers to Stay Home During Holy Week
item.description: Surging gasoline prices in the Philippines have forced some people to cancel or scale back the Visita Iglesia, a Holy Week tradition in which Catholics travel to seven churches.
item.dc:creator: Francesca Regalado and Ezra Acayan
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Filipinos heading to the provinces for the Holy Week lining up at a bus terminal in Quezon City, Philippines, on Thursday.

item.title: Iran Shoots Down U.S. Fighter Jet, Setting Off American Search and Rescue
item.description: One crew member was rescued. The risky operation to find the second airman came as the United States and Israel struck infrastructure targets in Iran, prompting Iranian retaliation in the Gulf.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt, Helene Cooper and Thomas Fuller
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Iran Is Quickly Repairing Missile Bunkers, U.S. Intelligence Says
item.description: Reports cast doubt on how close the United States is to destroying Iran’s missile capability, a key goal in the war.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes and Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: The tail section of a missile in Israel’s northern Jordan Valley on Friday. Iran has continued to strike at the country, even with its reduced arsenal and more careful use of launchers.

item.title: The F-15E Strike Eagle: What to Know
item.description: The U.S. military aircraft with a two-person crew was shot down in Iran on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: The U.S. aircraft that was brought down over Iran has been identified as an F-15E Strike Eagle. One of its two-member crew is still missing.

item.title: Hegseth Says U.S. Troops Are Fighting for Jesus. The Pope Disagrees.
item.description: In sharp contrast to the Trump administration’s calls for Christian prayers for the war effort, Pope Leo XIV says military domination is “entirely foreign to the way of Jesus Christ.”
item.dc:creator: Motoko Rich
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Pope Leo, during the Holy Thursday Mass in Rome, said the Christian mission had often been “distorted by a desire for domination.”

item.title: What U.S. military Pilots Are Trained to Do if Shot Down
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: American military pilots are trained in a set of principles called Survival, Evasion, Resistance and Escape to prepare for the possibility of being shot down in hostile territory.

item.title: Iranian media outlets say helicopters are searching for the crew of the downed jet.
item.dc:creator: Leily Nikounazar and Yeganeh Torbati

item.title: Israel plans to seize control of parts of southern Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Christina Goldbaum, Samuel Granados and Sanjana Varghese

item.title: Kuwait Blames Iran for Attack on Power and Desalination Plants
item.description: In Kuwait, an Iranian attack on Friday damaged a power and water desalination plant, officials said. Both sides in the Iran war have ramped up strikes on civilian targets.
item.dc:creator: Aurelien Breeden
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Smoke rising from Kuwait’s international airport after a reported drone strike on Wednesday.

item.title: How Do You Count 1.4 Billion People? India Is Trying.
item.description: India’s 2027 census will shape how wealth and power is distributed and inform policies around castes and women in the world’s most populous nation.
item.dc:creator: Pragati K.B. and John Yoon
item.media:description: An official assisting a woman with her online census entry on Wednesday at a center in Srinagar, part of Indian-administered Kashmir.

item.title: At War With Iran Again, Some Israelis Fear Conflict Is Becoming Routine
item.description: A majority of Israelis support the war with Iran, but many doubt that it will solve Israel’s long-term security problems. Some also question their prime minister’s assurances and motives.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Kershner
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Avishag Shaar-Yashuv for The New York Times
item.media:description: Residents standing near their building, which was damaged in a missile strike, in Tel Aviv last month.

item.title: Could There Be a Popular Uprising in Cuba?
item.description: There is no organized opposition on the island because many critics of the regime have fled. But anti-government protests have been growing.
item.dc:creator: David C. Adams and Annie Correal
item.media:description: A screen grab from a social media post showing protesters outside the Communist Party headquarters in the Cuban city of Morón in March.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt, Helene Cooper, Yeganeh Torbati, Adam Rasgon and Ronen Bergman

item.title: Myanmar Junta Chief Ascends to President, Five Years After Coup
item.description: U Min Aung Hlaing’s elevation to the civilian post is the conclusion of elections in the country, which were stage managed by the military.
item.dc:creator: Richard C. Paddock
item.media:description: Myanmar’s military ruler, Min Aung Hlaing, after casting hist vote in Naypyidaw, the capital, in December. He was elected president on Friday by the country’s rubber-stamp Parliament.

item.title: Cosmopolitan Shanghai Is an Uneasy Fit in China’s Narrative of Western Sins
item.description: Shanghai’s many layers of architecture, culture and politics have made it a difficult fit for the Communist Party’s preferred narrative of Chinese victimhood and Western sins.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Higgins and Qilai Shen
item.media:description: The Shanghai History Museum, housed in the former Shanghai Race Club.

item.title: In Syria, Kidnappings of Women and Girls Fuel a Minority Group’s Fears
item.description: A Times investigation found that abductions of women and girls from Syria’s Alawite minority were more common, and more brutal, than the government has acknowledged.
item.dc:creator: Ben Hubbard and Laura Boushnak
item.media:description: A 16-year-old girl who said she was kidnapped and raped, and later found out she was pregnant.

item.title: Cuba Says It Is Pardoning More Than 2,000 Prisoners
item.description: The Cuban government said the releases were a humanitarian gesture during Holy Week. It was not clear if they were related to ongoing negotiations between Cuba and the United States.
item.dc:creator: Francesca Regalado and David C. Adams
item.media:description: Havana in January. The Cuban Embassy in Washington described the latest pardons as a “humanitarian and sovereign gesture.”

item.title: Trump’s Strait of Hormuz Problem
item.description: Our White House reporter Zolan Kanno-Youngs parses some of President Trump’s conflicting messages about the war with Iran.
item.dc:creator: Zolan Kanno-Youngs, Christina Thornell, Coleman Lowndes and Whitney Shefte
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Thursday
item.description: President Trump’s prime-time address received a defiant response from a top leader in Tehran.
item.dc:creator: Anushka Patil
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Amit Elkayam for The New York Times
item.media:description: Workers inspecting the damage from a strike by Iran on Petah Tikva, Israel, on Thursday.

item.title: Macron Voices Europe’s Frustration With Trump’s Handling of Iran War
item.description: President Emmanuel Macron of France expressed disapproval about President Trump’s handling of the war against Iran on Thursday, chastising him for speaking cavalierly in a speech.
item.dc:creator: Mark Landler, Erika Solomon and Thomas Fuller
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Emmanuel Macron of France in Seoul on Thursday. Addressing President Trump’s verbal attacks against NATO, he said, “If you create doubt every day about your commitment, you hollow it out.”

item.title: Iranians hold a nature festival in the shadow of war.
item.description: Typically on Sizdah Bedar, families picnic outside, gather around rivers or streams, tell stories and take part in simple traditions that bind them to their heritage.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati and Sanam Mahoozi
